questions: how to fine tune a model?
fine tuning vs training from scratch?
answers: Fine-tuning means taking a pre-trained model and training it further on your specific dataset. It's faster and cheaper than training from scratch because the model already knows general patterns.
Fine-tuning holo ekta pre-trained model ke specific task er jonno customize kora. Jemon, GPT ke medical data diye fine-tune korle medical chatbot banano jay.
Model fine-tuning adjusts a pre-trained model's weights using domain-specific data. Techniques like LoRA and QLoRA make it efficient by only updating a small subset of parameters.
Fine-tuning is essential when you need a model specialized for your use case. Instead of training billions of parameters from scratch, you adapt an existing model with much less data and compute.
